target.name: Dexterity
target.founder: Samir Menon, Robert Sun, Kevin Chavez, Ben Varkey Benjamin, Talbot Morris-Downing, Cuthbert Sun

methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $1.6B last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 5 same-sector + same-stage peers in Robotics & Manufacturing. Range: $300M - $10.0B.

private.name: 1X Technologies
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series B), post-money $10B

private.name: Bedrock Robotics
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series B), post-money $1.75B

private.name: Collaborative Robotics
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series B), post-money $500M+

private.name: Fairmat
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series B), post-money $350M

private.name: GrayMatter Robotics
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series B), post-money $300M+

data.narrative:
Dexterity, a Series B robotics and manufacturing company founded by Samir Menon and others, carries a current post-money valuation of $1.65 billion after raising $291 million to date. The valuation assessment draws on a comp universe of nine private firms at the same stage—including 1X Technologies ($10.0B), Bedrock Robotics ($1.8B), Collaborative Robotics ($500M), Fairmat ($350M), and GrayMatter Robotics ($300M)—plus one public comparable.

Two triangulation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis, using a 1.9x multiplier typical of Series B advancement, implies a forward valuation of $3.1 billion from the $1.6 billion base. Peer-median analysis across the five named private peers yields a median valuation of $500 million, with the comp set ranging from $300 million to $10.0 billion. The wide spread reflects material heterogeneity within the robotics sector at this stage.

Combining both methods produces a triangulated range of $425 million (low) to $3.6 billion (high), with a median of $1.8 billion. The current $1.65 billion valuation sits slightly below this median, close to the peer median point. Key uncertainties include the significant valuation dispersion among peers—driven partly by differentiated technology risk and market positioning—and the limited applicability of public comps to this early-stage, venture-backed cohort. The analysis supports a range anchored near current valuation, though downside to $425–500 million and upside to $3.1–3.6 billion remain plausible depending on execution and capital market conditions.
